category (P373) == * Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '広島県府中市出口町745', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} [[JA:甘南備神社 (広島県府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q104660635}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kannabi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}{{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}} Deguchi-cho 745 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 1 ({{jalink|708|Q23511}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kannabi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ichi-no-torii-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kannabi Shrine''' (かんなびじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in Deguchi-cho, {{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}}, {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === In {{jalink|708|Q23511}} (Wado 1), when an epidemic spread, Saeki Sukune Maro, then provincial governor of {{jalink|Bingo Province|Q1049113}}, is said to have invited a divided spirit of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto||Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} from {{jalink|Miho Shrine|Q3313076}} in {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}. The epidemic was driven away through prayer, and in gratitude {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} were additionally enshrined. The shrine gained reverence from officials of the {{jalink|Bingo provincial office|Q28682318}}, rose in status, and on {{jalink|November 13, 878|Q23910}} ({{jalink|Genkei|Q747451}} 2), received promotion from Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} to "Senior Fifth Rank Upper," the highest in Bingo Province. Its name also appears in the Ashida District section of the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}''. Because this shrine stands on an ancient transport route linking Sanyo and San'in, it is said that people enshrined an Izumo-lineage deity here in contrast to the Kibi-lineage Nangu Shrine (Kurikara-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima)Shigeru Morimoto, 1995, p. 250. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi||Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) * {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} ({{jalink|Onamuchi-no-Kami}}) * {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} * Aiden deity: Ryuo deity === History === * {{jalink|708 in Japan|Q56346742|708}} (Wado 1) - Founded. * {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) - Relocated and merged here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} following an oracle of the Ryuo deity. * {{jalink|760|Q30552}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307}} 4) - During an epidemic, provincial governor Kannabi Mahito Ika prayed and received divine efficacy; granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9, 4th month, 8th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Seiwa|Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{jalink|878|Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|1558|Q6601}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{jalink|Mori Motonari|Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{jalink|1638|Q6893}} ({{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}} 15) - {{jalink|Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{jalink|1703|Q6822}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{jalink|1706|Q6835}} ({{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{jalink|Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123}} of {{jalink|Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{jalink|1734|Q6977}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{jalink|1764|Q7659}} ({{jalink|Meiwa|Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{jalink|1789|Q7772}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{jalink|1866|Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{jalink|1878|Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1924|Q2173}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{jalink|1958|Q5253}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} in {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{jalink|Masana Murakami|Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Hiroshima)}} [[JA:甘南備神社 (広島県府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q104660635}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kannabi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}{{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}} Deguchi-cho 745 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 1 ({{jalink|708|Q23511}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kannabi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ichi-no-torii-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kannabi Shrine''' (かんなびじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in Deguchi-cho, {{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}}, {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === In {{jalink|708|Q23511}} (Wado 1), when an epidemic spread, Saeki Sukune Maro, then provincial governor of {{jalink|Bingo Province|Q1049113}}, is said to have invited a divided spirit of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto||Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} from {{jalink|Miho Shrine|Q3313076}} in {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}. The epidemic was driven away through prayer, and in gratitude {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} were additionally enshrined. The shrine gained reverence from officials of the {{jalink|Bingo provincial office|Q28682318}}, rose in status, and on {{jalink|November 13, 878|Q23910}} ({{jalink|Genkei|Q747451}} 2), received promotion from Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} to "Senior Fifth Rank Upper," the highest in Bingo Province. Its name also appears in the Ashida District section of the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}''. Because this shrine stands on an ancient transport route linking Sanyo and San'in, it is said that people enshrined an Izumo-lineage deity here in contrast to the Kibi-lineage Nangu Shrine (Kurikara-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima)Shigeru Morimoto, 1995, p. 250. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi||Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) * {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} ({{jalink|Onamuchi-no-Kami}}) * {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} * Aiden deity: Ryuo deity === History === * {{jalink|708 in Japan|Q56346742|708}} (Wado 1) - Founded. * {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) - Relocated and merged here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} following an oracle of the Ryuo deity. * {{jalink|760|Q30552}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307}} 4) - During an epidemic, provincial governor Kannabi Mahito Ika prayed and received divine efficacy; granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9, 4th month, 8th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Seiwa|Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{jalink|878|Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|1558|Q6601}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{jalink|Mori Motonari|Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{jalink|1638|Q6893}} ({{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}} 15) - {{jalink|Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{jalink|1703|Q6822}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{jalink|1706|Q6835}} ({{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{jalink|Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123}} of {{jalink|Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{jalink|1734|Q6977}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{jalink|1764|Q7659}} ({{jalink|Meiwa|Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{jalink|1789|Q7772}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{jalink|1866|Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{jalink|1878|Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1924|Q2173}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{jalink|1958|Q5253}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} in {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{jalink|Masana Murakami|Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Hiroshima)}} [[JA:甘南備神社 (広島県府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q104660635}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kannabi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}{{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}} Deguchi-cho 745 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 1 ({{jalink|708|Q23511}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kannabi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ichi-no-torii-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kannabi Shrine''' (かんなびじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in Deguchi-cho, {{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}}, {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === In {{jalink|708|Q23511}} (Wado 1), when an epidemic spread, Saeki Sukune Maro, then provincial governor of {{jalink|Bingo Province|Q1049113}}, is said to have invited a divided spirit of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto||Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} from {{jalink|Miho Shrine|Q3313076}} in {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}. The epidemic was driven away through prayer, and in gratitude {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} were additionally enshrined. The shrine gained reverence from officials of the {{jalink|Bingo provincial office|Q28682318}}, rose in status, and on {{jalink|November 13, 878|Q23910}} ({{jalink|Genkei|Q747451}} 2), received promotion from Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} to "Senior Fifth Rank Upper," the highest in Bingo Province. Its name also appears in the Ashida District section of the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}''. Because this shrine stands on an ancient transport route linking Sanyo and San'in, it is said that people enshrined an Izumo-lineage deity here in contrast to the Kibi-lineage Nangu Shrine (Kurikara-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima)Shigeru Morimoto, 1995, p. 250. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi||Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) * {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} ({{jalink|Onamuchi-no-Kami}}) * {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} * Aiden deity: Ryuo deity === History === * {{jalink|708 in Japan|Q56346742|708}} (Wado 1) - Founded. * {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) - Relocated and merged here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} following an oracle of the Ryuo deity. * {{jalink|760|Q30552}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307}} 4) - During an epidemic, provincial governor Kannabi Mahito Ika prayed and received divine efficacy; granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9, 4th month, 8th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Seiwa|Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{jalink|878|Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|1558|Q6601}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{jalink|Mori Motonari|Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{jalink|1638|Q6893}} ({{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}} 15) - {{jalink|Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{jalink|1703|Q6822}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{jalink|1706|Q6835}} ({{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{jalink|Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123}} of {{jalink|Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{jalink|1734|Q6977}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{jalink|1764|Q7659}} ({{jalink|Meiwa|Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{jalink|1789|Q7772}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{jalink|1866|Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{jalink|1878|Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1924|Q2173}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{jalink|1958|Q5253}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} in {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{jalink|Masana Murakami|Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{jalink|1878|Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1924|Q2173}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{jalink|1958|Q5253}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} in {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{jalink|Masana Murakami|Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]]

Hiroshima)}} [[JA:甘南備神社 (広島県府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q104660635}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kannabi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}{{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}} Deguchi-cho 745 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 1 ({{jalink|708|Q23511}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kannabi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ichi-no-torii-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kannabi Shrine''' (かんなびじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in Deguchi-cho, {{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}}, {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === In {{jalink|708|Q23511}} (Wado 1), when an epidemic spread, Saeki Sukune Maro, then provincial governor of {{jalink|Bingo Province|Q1049113}}, is said to have invited a divided spirit of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto||Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} from {{jalink|Miho Shrine|Q3313076}} in {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}. The epidemic was driven away through prayer, and in gratitude {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} were additionally enshrined. The shrine gained reverence from officials of the {{jalink|Bingo provincial office|Q28682318}}, rose in status, and on {{jalink|November 13, 878|Q23910}} ({{jalink|Genkei|Q747451}} 2), received promotion from Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} to "Senior Fifth Rank Upper," the highest in Bingo Province. Its name also appears in the Ashida District section of the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}''. Because this shrine stands on an ancient transport route linking Sanyo and San'in, it is said that people enshrined an Izumo-lineage deity here in contrast to the Kibi-lineage Nangu Shrine (Kurikara-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima)Shigeru Morimoto, 1995, p. 250. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi||Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) * {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} ({{jalink|Onamuchi-no-Kami}}) * {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} * Aiden deity: Ryuo deity === History === * {{jalink|708 in Japan|Q56346742|708}} (Wado 1) - Founded. * {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) - Relocated and merged here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} following an oracle of the Ryuo deity. * {{jalink|760|Q30552}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307}} 4) - During an epidemic, provincial governor Kannabi Mahito Ika prayed and received divine efficacy; granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9, 4th month, 8th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Seiwa|Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{jalink|878|Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|1558|Q6601}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{jalink|Mori Motonari|Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{jalink|1638|Q6893}} ({{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}} 15) - {{jalink|Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{jalink|1703|Q6822}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{jalink|1706|Q6835}} ({{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{jalink|Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123}} of {{jalink|Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{jalink|1734|Q6977}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{jalink|1764|Q7659}} ({{jalink|Meiwa|Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{jalink|1789|Q7772}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{jalink|1866|Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{jalink|1878|Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1924|Q2173}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{jalink|1958|Q5253}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} in {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{jalink|Masana Murakami|Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 1 ({{jalink|708|Q23511}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kannabi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ichi-no-torii-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kannabi Shrine''' (かんなびじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in Deguchi-cho, {{jalink|Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|Fuchu}}, {{jalink|Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === In {{jalink|708|Q23511}} (Wado 1), when an epidemic spread, Saeki Sukune Maro, then provincial governor of {{jalink|Bingo Province|Q1049113}}, is said to have invited a divided spirit of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto||Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} from {{jalink|Miho Shrine|Q3313076}} in {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}. The epidemic was driven away through prayer, and in gratitude {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} were additionally enshrined. The shrine gained reverence from officials of the {{jalink|Bingo provincial office|Q28682318}}, rose in status, and on {{jalink|November 13, 878|Q23910}} ({{jalink|Genkei|Q747451}} 2), received promotion from Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} to "Senior Fifth Rank Upper," the highest in Bingo Province. Its name also appears in the Ashida District section of the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}''. Because this shrine stands on an ancient transport route linking Sanyo and San'in, it is said that people enshrined an Izumo-lineage deity here in contrast to the Kibi-lineage Nangu Shrine (Kurikara-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima)Shigeru Morimoto, 1995, p. 250. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi||Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) * {{jalink|Okuninushi-no-Kami}} ({{jalink|Onamuchi-no-Kami}}) * {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Kami}} * Aiden deity: Ryuo deity === History === * {{jalink|708 in Japan|Q56346742|708}} (Wado 1) - Founded. * {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) - Relocated and merged here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} following an oracle of the Ryuo deity. * {{jalink|760|Q30552}} ({{jalink|Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307}} 4) - During an epidemic, provincial governor Kannabi Mahito Ika prayed and received divine efficacy; granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|867|Q30347}} ({{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 9, 4th month, 8th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Seiwa|Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{jalink|878|Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{jalink|Yozei|Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{jalink|1558|Q6601}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{jalink|Mori Motonari|Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{jalink|1638|Q6893}} ({{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}} 15) - {{jalink|Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{jalink|1703|Q6822}} ({{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{jalink|1706|Q6835}} ({{jalink|Hoei|Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{jalink|Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123}} of {{jalink|Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{jalink|1734|Q6977}} ({{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{jalink|1764|Q7659}} ({{jalink|Meiwa|Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{jalink|1789|Q7772}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{jalink|1866|Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{jalink|1878|Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{jalink|1924|Q2173}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{jalink|1958|Q5253}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{jalink|Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|Fujio Village}} in {{jalink|723 in Japan|Q56347391|723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{jalink|Masana Murakami|Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Hiroshima)}} [[JA:甘南備神社 (広島県府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q104660635}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kannabi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}{{ill|lt=Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|WD=Q686775|lt=Fuchu}} Deguchi-cho 745 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill||lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 1 ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kannabi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ichi-no-torii-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kannabi Shrine''' (かんなびじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Deguchi-cho, {{ill|lt=Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|WD=Q686775|lt=Fuchu}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill||lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In {{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511}} (Wado 1), when an epidemic spread, Saeki Sukune Maro, then provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Bingo Province|Q1049113|WD=Q1049113}}, is said to have invited a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} from {{ill|lt=Miho Shrine|Q3313076|WD=Q3313076}} in {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}. The epidemic was driven away through prayer, and in gratitude {{ill||lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill||lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} were additionally enshrined. The shrine gained reverence from officials of the {{ill|lt=Bingo provincial office|Q28682318|WD=Q28682318}}, rose in status, and on {{ill|lt=November 13, 878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}} ({{ill|lt=Genkei|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 2), received promotion from Emperor {{ill|lt=Yozei|Q349280|WD=Q349280}} to "Senior Fifth Rank Upper," the highest in Bingo Province. Its name also appears in the Ashida District section of the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''. Because this shrine stands on an ancient transport route linking Sanyo and San'in, it is said that people enshrined an Izumo-lineage deity here in contrast to the Kibi-lineage Nangu Shrine (Kurikara-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima)Shigeru Morimoto, 1995, p. 250. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi||WD=|lt=Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) * {{ill||lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill||lt=Onamuchi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill||lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} * Aiden deity: Ryuo deity === History === * {{ill|lt=708 in Japan|Q56346742|WD=Q56346742|lt=708}} (Wado 1) - Founded. * {{ill|lt=723 in Japan|Q56347391|WD=Q56347391|lt=723}} (Yoro 7) - Relocated and merged here from {{ill|lt=Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|WD=Q11623827|lt=Fujio Village}} following an oracle of the Ryuo deity. * {{ill|lt=760|Q30552|WD=Q30552}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 4) - During an epidemic, provincial governor Kannabi Mahito Ika prayed and received divine efficacy; granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9, 4th month, 8th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{ill|lt=Seiwa|Q349243|WD=Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{ill|lt=Yozei|Q349280|WD=Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{ill|lt=Mori Motonari|Q1156545|WD=Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{ill|lt=1638|Q6893|WD=Q6893}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 15) - {{ill|lt=Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277|WD=Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{ill|lt=1703|Q6822|WD=Q6822}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{ill|lt=1706|Q6835|WD=Q6835}} ({{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123|WD=Q11239123}} of {{ill|lt=Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278|WD=Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{ill|lt=1734|Q6977|WD=Q6977}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{ill|lt=Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|WD=Q11623827|lt=Fujio Village}} in {{ill|lt=723 in Japan|Q56347391|WD=Q56347391|lt=723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|WD=Q5506963|lt=Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{ill|lt=Masana Murakami|Q17231346|WD=Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Promoted by Emperor {{ill|lt=Seiwa|Q349243|WD=Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{ill|lt=Yozei|Q349280|WD=Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{ill|lt=Mori Motonari|Q1156545|WD=Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{ill|lt=1638|Q6893|WD=Q6893}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 15) - {{ill|lt=Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277|WD=Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{ill|lt=1703|Q6822|WD=Q6822}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{ill|lt=1706|Q6835|WD=Q6835}} ({{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123|WD=Q11239123}} of {{ill|lt=Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278|WD=Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{ill|lt=1734|Q6977|WD=Q6977}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{ill|lt=Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|WD=Q11623827|lt=Fujio Village}} in {{ill|lt=723 in Japan|Q56347391|WD=Q56347391|lt=723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|WD=Q5506963|lt=Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{ill|lt=Masana Murakami|Q17231346|WD=Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Hiroshima)}} [[JA:甘南備神社 (広島県府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q104660635}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kannabi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}{{ill|lt=Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|WD=Q686775|lt=Fuchu}} Deguchi-cho 745 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 1 ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kannabi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ichi-no-torii-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kannabi Shrine''' (かんなびじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Deguchi-cho, {{ill|lt=Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|WD=Q686775|lt=Fuchu}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In {{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511}} (Wado 1), when an epidemic spread, Saeki Sukune Maro, then provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Bingo Province|Q1049113|WD=Q1049113}}, is said to have invited a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} from {{ill|lt=Miho Shrine|Q3313076|WD=Q3313076}} in {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}. The epidemic was driven away through prayer, and in gratitude {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} were additionally enshrined. The shrine gained reverence from officials of the {{ill|lt=Bingo provincial office|Q28682318|WD=Q28682318}}, rose in status, and on {{ill|lt=November 13, 878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}} ({{ill|lt=Genkei|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 2), received promotion from Emperor {{ill|lt=Yozei|Q349280|WD=Q349280}} to "Senior Fifth Rank Upper," the highest in Bingo Province. Its name also appears in the Ashida District section of the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''. Because this shrine stands on an ancient transport route linking Sanyo and San'in, it is said that people enshrined an Izumo-lineage deity here in contrast to the Kibi-lineage Nangu Shrine (Kurikara-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima)Shigeru Morimoto, 1995, p. 250. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi||WD=|lt=Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Aiden deity: Ryuo deity === History === * {{ill|lt=708 in Japan|Q56346742|WD=Q56346742|lt=708}} (Wado 1) - Founded. * {{ill|lt=723 in Japan|Q56347391|WD=Q56347391|lt=723}} (Yoro 7) - Relocated and merged here from {{ill|lt=Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|WD=Q11623827|lt=Fujio Village}} following an oracle of the Ryuo deity. * {{ill|lt=760|Q30552|WD=Q30552}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 4) - During an epidemic, provincial governor Kannabi Mahito Ika prayed and received divine efficacy; granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9, 4th month, 8th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{ill|lt=Seiwa|Q349243|WD=Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{ill|lt=Yozei|Q349280|WD=Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{ill|lt=Mori Motonari|Q1156545|WD=Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{ill|lt=1638|Q6893|WD=Q6893}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 15) - {{ill|lt=Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277|WD=Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{ill|lt=1703|Q6822|WD=Q6822}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{ill|lt=1706|Q6835|WD=Q6835}} ({{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123|WD=Q11239123}} of {{ill|lt=Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278|WD=Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{ill|lt=1734|Q6977|WD=Q6977}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{ill|lt=Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|WD=Q11623827|lt=Fujio Village}} in {{ill|lt=723 in Japan|Q56347391|WD=Q56347391|lt=723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|WD=Q5506963|lt=Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{ill|lt=Masana Murakami|Q17231346|WD=Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Hiroshima)}} [[JA:甘南備神社 (広島県府中市)]] {{wikidata link|Q104660635}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kannabi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}{{ill|lt=Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|WD=Q686775|lt=Fuchu}} Deguchi-cho 745 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 1 ({{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Kannabi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ichi-no-torii-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Kannabi Shrine''' (かんなびじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Deguchi-cho, {{ill|lt=Fuchu (Hiroshima)|Q686775|WD=Q686775|lt=Fuchu}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|WD=Q617375}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In {{ill|lt=708|Q23511|WD=Q23511}} (Wado 1), when an epidemic spread, Saeki Sukune Maro, then provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Bingo Province|Q1049113|WD=Q1049113}}, is said to have invited a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} from {{ill|lt=Miho Shrine|Q3313076|WD=Q3313076}} in {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}. The epidemic was driven away through prayer, and in gratitude {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} were additionally enshrined. The shrine gained reverence from officials of the {{ill|lt=Bingo provincial office|Q28682318|WD=Q28682318}}, rose in status, and on {{ill|lt=November 13, 878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}} ({{ill|lt=Genkei|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 2), received promotion from Emperor {{ill|lt=Yozei|Q349280|WD=Q349280}} to "Senior Fifth Rank Upper," the highest in Bingo Province. Its name also appears in the Ashida District section of the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''. Because this shrine stands on an ancient transport route linking Sanyo and San'in, it is said that people enshrined an Izumo-lineage deity here in contrast to the Kibi-lineage Nangu Shrine (Kurikara-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima)Shigeru Morimoto, 1995, p. 250. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Tsumihayae Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Aiden deity: Ryuo deity === History === * {{ill|lt=708 in Japan|Q56346742|WD=Q56346742|lt=708}} (Wado 1) - Founded. * {{ill|lt=723 in Japan|Q56347391|WD=Q56347391|lt=723}} (Yoro 7) - Relocated and merged here from {{ill|lt=Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|WD=Q11623827|lt=Fujio Village}} following an oracle of the Ryuo deity. * {{ill|lt=760|Q30552|WD=Q30552}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 4) - During an epidemic, provincial governor Kannabi Mahito Ika prayed and received divine efficacy; granted Junior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 9, 4th month, 8th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{ill|lt=Seiwa|Q349243|WD=Q349243}} from Bingo Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower. * {{ill|lt=878|Q23910|WD=Q23910}} (Genkei 2, 11th month, 13th day) - Promoted by Emperor {{ill|lt=Yozei|Q349280|WD=Q349280}} from Bingo Senior Fifth Rank Lower to Senior Fifth Rank Upper. * {{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 1) - In a great drought, {{ill|lt=Mori Motonari|Q1156545|WD=Q1156545}} offered rain-prayer thanks and donated one cho of rice field in perpetuity. * {{ill|lt=1638|Q6893|WD=Q6893}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 15) - {{ill|lt=Mizuno Katsunari|Q8190277|WD=Q8190277}} performed rain-prayer thanks and donated one parcel of field for offerings. * {{ill|lt=1703|Q6822|WD=Q6822}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} 16) - Subsidiary Ryuo Shrine built. * {{ill|lt=1706|Q6835|WD=Q6835}} ({{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 3, 8th month) - Lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Tadamasa|Q11239123|WD=Q11239123}} of {{ill|lt=Bingo Fukuyama Domain|Q909278|WD=Q909278}} built the main hall. * {{ill|lt=1734|Q6977|WD=Q6977}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 19, 6th month) - Haiden built. * {{ill|lt=1764|Q7659|WD=Q7659}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161}} 1, 5th-9th months) - Main hall roof rethatched. * {{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 1) - Repairs. * {{ill|lt=1866|Q7712|WD=Q7712}} (Keio 2, 4th month, 8th day) - 1000th anniversary festival of Seiwa Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5) - Promoted to gosha. * {{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{ill|lt=Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|WD=Q11623827|lt=Fujio Village}} in {{ill|lt=723 in Japan|Q56347391|WD=Q56347391|lt=723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|WD=Q5506963|lt=Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{ill|lt=Masana Murakami|Q17231346|WD=Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Promoted to gosha. * {{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}} (Meiji 11), Dec 17 - 1000th anniversary festival of Yozei Emperor's rank grant held. * {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13) - Haiden rebuilt. * Oct 19, 1928 (Showa 3) - Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|WD=Q5253}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 33) - Treasure hall and Mimuro Hall newly built as part of 1250th enshrinement anniversary project. === Precincts === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shaden-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}]] * Main hall * Worship hall * Offering preparation place * Inari Shrine * Mimuro Hall * Kagura hall * Purification basin shelter * Shrine office * Zuijin gate * Treasure hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), shinmon.jpg|Zuijin gate File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), ni-no-torii.jpg|Second torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima), keidaisha-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Precinct shrines}}]] * Ryuo Shrine - said to have been relocated here from {{ill|lt=Fujio Village (Hiroshima)|Q11623827|WD=Q11623827|lt=Fujio Village}} in {{ill|lt=723 in Japan|Q56347391|WD=Q56347391|lt=723}} (Yoro 7) during drought by oracle of the Ryuo deity. * Taishi Shrine * Kojin Shrine * Amenomahitotsu Shrine * Kinoyama Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Chufu Shrine * Yabu Shrine * Inari-jin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tamamori Inari Shrine === Festivals === * Kannabi Shrine dedication children's sumo tournament * Grand annual festival mikoshi procession * Nagoshi great purification ring rite === Site information === === Location === 745 Deguchi-cho, Fuchu, Hiroshima === Access === About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Fuchu Station (Hiroshima)|Q5506963|WD=Q5506963|lt=Fuchu Station}} === Footnotes === === Bibliography === * Tokuichi Kano, ''Kannabi Shrine Chokoroku'', 1898 (in ''Bingo Shidan'') * {{ill|lt=Masana Murakami|Q17231346|WD=Q17231346}}, ''Folklore and Tales of Binnan: Hometown History'', Toyo Shoin, 1992 * Shigeru Morimoto, ''History Walks of Bingo (Upper)'', Sanyo Shimbunsha, 1995 * Hiroshima Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: https://www.hiroshima-jinjacho.jp/ === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Kan-nabi-jinja (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんなひしんしや ひろしまけんふちゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kannabi Shrine (Fuchu city, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県府中市の建築物]] [[Category:広島県府中市の歴史]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]
